senses.gloss: to prop up, to assist, to support

examples.text: وَمَثَلُهُمْ فِي ٱلْإِنْجِيلِ كَزَرْعٍ أَخْرَجَ شَطْأَهُ فَآزَرَهُ فَٱسْتَغْلَظَ فَٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَىٰ سُوقِهِ

senses.gloss: the caretaker of Abraham, believed by Sunnis to be his father (i.e. Terah) and by Shi'ites to be his paternal uncle.

examples.text: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ آزَرَ أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْنَامًا آلِهَةً إِنِّي أَرَاكَ وَقَوْمَكَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
examples.english: Remember when Abraham said to his father Āzar: Takest thou idols for gods? Aye! I see thee and thy folk in error manifest.

etymology.text:
Denominal verb of إِزَار (ʔizār, “drawers”). Compare equipped weightlifting.

From Hebrew אֱלִיעֶזֶר (ʾelī-ʿezer, “Eliezer”), same source as of عَازِر (ʕāzir, “Ezra”).
